planets.name: Good
planets.summary: Archive cluster around good: 324 posts; 4 quote-backed receipts pinned.

artifacts.title: Good receipt from 2024-07-18
artifacts.excerpt:
New paper, listing 43 ways ML evaluations can be misleading or actively deceptive. 

Following the good critics of psychological science we call these "questionable research practices" (QRPs). (The working title was "How To Lie In Machine Learning") https://t.co/x2WziJ2Fqz
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: good, really, something, bad.

artifacts.title: Good receipt from 2024-04-20
artifacts.excerpt: Twitter follow is a good game. If you play well, you grow much smarter than you could possibly become on your own. If you play badly you completely lose your mind, become a shell of yourself, endure the penal colony of fame with none of its upsides.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: good, really, something, bad.

artifacts.title: Good receipt from 2023-12-03
artifacts.excerpt:
Good news for the year 2323: rigorous sim of a new kind of computer not limited by the usual physical constraints and close to reversible (i.e. free operations).

Biggest story of the year on the long view

https://t.co/sJ6zlsEurI
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: good, really, something, bad.

artifacts.title: Good receipt from 2024-10-19
artifacts.excerpt:
in hindsight an obvious perspective: classify companies by what the principals use the surplus on

Stripe: good writing
Google: lots of stuff. e.g. Waymo, amazing quantum computers
Jane Street: unusual charities 
Epic Games: programming languages?
Amazon: self-optimisation

A common proxy for wearing, government mask mandates, don't correlate with large increases in wearing in our window. And so we don’t find evidence that mandates reduce transmission. 

This is methodological: not "mandates don’t work" but "mandate data don’t tell us if they work" https://t.co/Ja4qt7RFO4

artifacts.title: Most receipt from 2023-10-08
artifacts.excerpt:
The billions of hours that anon randos spent writing most of the public internet sure seemed like a frivolous use of a life. But their reward was huge, a capital-L legacy: 

extremely disproportionate representation in the weights of the coming machines.

https://t.co/Av48Hx5X9K
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: most, time, important, world.

artifacts.title: Most receipt from 2023-12-03
artifacts.excerpt:
I was disappointed in last year's malaria vaccine (RTS,S) but R21 might be the real deal: best guess is ~80% protection (nonrobust, sample had very few deaths). way more scalable too.

One of humanity's oldest, most pitiless enemies, on his way out.

https://t.co/xxLGxs2anS
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: most, time, important, world.

artifacts.title: Most receipt from 2025-06-26
artifacts.excerpt:
Michel Foucault is thought to be the world's most cited academic, with >1,440,000 citations. 

But Geoffrey Hinton has been catching up, accelerating where Foucault is decelerating in the last 5 years. When will Hinton overtake Foucault - when is the Moment of Hintotality? https://t.co/UsYdPCNrXU
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: most, time, important, world.

artifacts.title: Most receipt from 2025-07-03
artifacts.excerpt: Famously, Leibniz wanted a universal symbolic language  for all concepts *with a complete solver* such that e.g. religious debates could be algorithmically permanently resolved. Despite its impossibility, this is one of the all-time most amazing thoughts.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: most, time, important, world.

artifacts.title: Time receipt from 2022-10-16
artifacts.excerpt:
- Huge rate of nondeterministic semantic errors at query time.
- Catastrophic "success". No ability to reject bad or empty queries.
- LM query engineering (prompting) currently not a science. Compare the awesome power and reliability of RDB query optimisers.
